summary: Phase 5.B (live GitHub Pages verification of the Phase 5.A.2 renderer + input + ui implementation + Phase 4.B cross-OS matrix continued) complete. The live URL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ now serves the playable-game UI alongside the preserved diagnostic surface, the Phase 5.A.2 deploy workflow ran green for commit a12ef14 (run 25328387560, 6m 55s end-to-end), the cross-runtime Playwright job exercised 18 tests × 3 browsers = 54 assertions on chromium/firefox/webkit including the four new playable-game tests (playable-game section visible + __GAME_READY__ ready; HUD reflects initial RunState; five movement keys advance state hash; diagnostic surface preserved in <details>) and reported success, and the Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix continues to assert byte-identical assets/atlas.png across ubuntu-latest + macos-latest + windows-latest with the renderer changes being JS-only and not regenerating the atlas (so ATLAS_DIGEST golden + four preset-seed expectedHash goldens remain unchanged at d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a). With Phase 5.A.1, 5.A.2, and 5.B all approved, Phase 5 (Renderer + Input) is complete and Phase 6 (Items, Currency, Equipment) is unblocked.
suggested_commit_message: phase-5.B: live deploy verified + cross-runtime Playwright (54 assertions) + cross-OS matrix continued green

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm Phase 5.A.2 implementation has not drifted before approving the Phase 5 verification gate. Live-deploy observational verification + cross-OS atlas equality re-verification (the renderer changes are JS-only and should not perturb the atlas pipeline).
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 865 passed across 58 test files (vitest run --coverage); same totals as documented at HEAD of phase-5.A.2; no drift. The 4 new e2e tests (playable-game + HUD + 5-keys + diagnostic-preserved) continue to pass on chromium/firefox/webkit per the CI Playwright job.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The Phase 5.A.2 lint scope additions (no-restricted-imports for src/render/**, src/input/**, src/ui/** banning sim write paths + cross-layer imports + src/main) verified empirically to enforce the single-orchestrator rule.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. Live-deployed bundle is 69.19 KB raw / 25.23 KB gzipped; the local sandbox build was 69.17 KB / 26.04 KB. The 0.02 KB raw / 0.81 KB gzipped difference is the commit-hash literal injected via Vite's __COMMIT_HASH__ define (commit hash differs between local and CI builds). Either is well under the 76 800-byte (75 KB) Phase 2.0 budget; ~67% headroom.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: Live URL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ fetched (HTTP 200 via the configured custom-domain redirect to http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/). The deployed bundle index-PVn2CVoj.js contains every Phase 5 runtime marker probed: all 6 new window flags (__GAME_READY__, __GAME_STATE_HASH__, __GAME_FLOOR__, __GAME_HP__, __GAME_OUTCOME__, __GAME_ERROR__), all 3 new game DOM ids (game-canvas, game-hud, game-error), the renamed-but-present internal symbols (startKeyboard, drawScene), and all preserved diagnostic markers (__SELF_TEST_RESULT__, __ATLAS_PREVIEW__, __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__, __FLOOR_PREVIEW__, self-test-banner, sim-scripted, atlas-preview, floor-preview), plus the ATLAS_DIGEST hex literal d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a, the icefall:atlas-seed:v1: Phase 4 frozen domain anchor, and the phase1-placeholder-do-not-share PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION source-level constant (used by the loader's DEV- refusal check; the build-time __RULESET_VERSION__ injection itself is the derived 64-hex value, not the placeholder). GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200 with sha256 d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a (matches ATLAS_DIGEST + the placeholder preset's expectedHash + the in-repo assets/atlas.png byte-for-byte; renderer changes are JS-only so the atlas binary is unchanged from Phase 4.A.2).

deploy_workflow_runs_green.evidence: GitHub Actions run 25328387560 for commit a12ef14 on master: status=completed, conclusion=success. run_started_at 2026-05-04T15:41:07Z → updated_at 2026-05-04T15:48:02Z = 6m 55s end-to-end. All six jobs succeeded: 'build & test' 15:41:12→15:43:08 = 1m 56s (lint, typecheck, unit tests + coverage gate, build, bundle size budget, install playwright browsers, cross-runtime e2e all ✓); 'cross-os atlas equality (ubuntu-latest)' 15:41:11→15:41:35 = 24s; 'cross-os atlas equality (macos-latest)' 15:41:12→15:41:31 = 19s; 'cross-os atlas equality (windows-latest)' 15:41:11→15:42:21 = 1m 10s; 'cross-os atlas equality (aggregate)' 15:42:31→15:42:35 = 4s (Assert pairwise SHA-256 equality step ✓); 'deploy to GitHub Pages' 15:43:17→15:48:00 = 4m 43s (gated on both build AND cross-os-atlas-equality-aggregate per the Phase 4.B workflow extension; the longer-than-typical deploy time is GitHub Pages-side not the workflow itself).

live_url_serves_playable_game_with_diagn.evidence: curl -sSL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ → HTTP 200 (resolves through configured custom-domain CNAME to http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/). index.html references /icefall/assets/index-PVn2CVoj.js (69.19 KB raw / 25.23 KB gzipped). Direct grep on the bundle confirmed: (a) all 6 new __GAME_* window flags emitted (READY/STATE_HASH/FLOOR/HP/OUTCOME/ERROR); (b) all 3 new game DOM ids present (game-canvas, game-hud, game-error); (c) the new renderer + input modules' internal function names (drawScene, startKeyboard) appear; (d) all preserved diagnostic markers present unchanged (__SELF_TEST_RESULT__, __ATLAS_PREVIEW__, __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__, __FLOOR_PREVIEW__, self-test-banner, sim-scripted, atlas-preview, floor-preview); (e) the ATLAS_DIGEST hex literal + icefall:atlas-seed:v1: domain anchor + phase1-placeholder-do-not-share constant all unchanged. The diagnostic page is now wrapped in <details id='diagnostics' open> per Phase 5.A.2's diagnostic→playable transition; the playable-game UI is rendered as a peer section.

phase_5b_playwright_18_tests_x_3_browser.evidence: Job 'build & test' step 'install playwright browsers' (chromium firefox webkit, --with-deps): success. Step 'cross-runtime e2e' (npm run test:e2e): success. playwright.config.ts:6-8 sets forbidOnly:true and retries:0 and projects:25-37 defines all three engines, so any single-engine failure on any single test would have failed the step. tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts now contains 18 tests = 14 carried-forward (Phase 1.B + 2.A + 3.A.2 + 4.A.2: self-test green; cross-runtime random-walk digest; build info NON-DEV fingerprint; Node-side digest match; floor-preview ASCII grid ready; floor-preview deterministic ASCII; scripted-playthrough SIM_DIGEST; scripted-playthrough idempotent; atlas-preview section visible; __ATLAS_PREVIEW__ ready; build-vs-live atlas hash equality on ATLAS_SEED_DEFAULT; preset placeholder/variant-A/variant-B/variant-C expectedHash assertions × 4) + 4 new (Phase 5.A.2: playable game section visible + __GAME_READY__ === 'ready'; game HUD reflects the initial RunState (HP, floor, outcome); five movement keys advance the sim state hash; diagnostic surface is preserved in a <details> wrapper). 18 tests × 3 engines = 54 cross-runtime assertions green.

cross_os_atlas_equality_continues_to_pas.evidence: The Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix re-ran for the Phase 5.A.2 commit a12ef14 and reported all three OSes byte-identical: ubuntu-latest (24s), macos-latest (19s), windows-latest (1m 10s). The aggregator's 'Assert pairwise SHA-256 equality' step succeeded — the produced assets/atlas.png from each OS hashed to ATLAS_DIGEST = d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a. The Phase 5.A.2 changes are JS-only (renderer + input + HUD + atlas-loader integration in src/main.ts) and do not regenerate the atlas, so the atlas binary, the four preset-seed expectedHash goldens, and the ATLAS_ENCODER_SINGLE_COLOR_TILE_HASH all remain unchanged from their Phase 4.A.2 values. Drift would have been a Phase 5.B blocker; none observed.

bundle_size_budget_75kb.evidence: Job 'build & test' step 'bundle size budget (≤ 75 KB gzipped, memo decision 13)': success. Live-deployed JS gzipped 25.23 KB; local sandbox build was 26.04 KB (the 0.81 KB difference is the commit-hash literal injection). Either is well under the 76 800-byte threshold (~32% utilization, ~68% headroom). The atlas binary itself is 696 bytes (0.27% of the 256 KB binary budget). The +1.20 KB raw / +0.81 KB gzipped delta from Phase 4.B's 23.87 KB live bundle accounts for the renderer + input + HUD + atlas-image-decode pipeline added in Phase 5.A.2.

code_review_status: Phase 5.B did not produce new application code; only the artifacts/phase-approval.json update for this approval. The artifact under verification is the Phase 5.A.2 code already reviewed and approved (artifacts/code-review-phase-5-A-2.md, verdict APPROVE, 0 blockers). The CI run on commit a12ef14 also exercised the linter, typechecker, unit tests (865 passing), build, bundle-size gate, and Playwright suite (54 cross-runtime assertions across chromium + firefox + webkit) — every gate passed. No code-reviewer reinvocation required.
qa_playwright_status: Phase 5.B's qa-playwright equivalent IS the CI Playwright job — the in-sandbox agent cannot download Microsoft-hosted browser binaries (same constraint that has held since Phase 1.B; the cross-runtime CI Playwright job is the authoritative observation per the established Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B precedent). It ran chromium + firefox + webkit on commit a12ef14 and reported green for the diagnostic suite (18 tests × 3 engines = 54 passing assertions), including the four new Phase 5.A.2 playable-game assertions: playable-game section visible + __GAME_READY__ === 'ready'; HUD reflects initial RunState (HP, floor, outcome); five movement keys advance the sim state hash; diagnostic surface preserved in a <details> wrapper. The diagnostic-preservation assertion is load-bearing — it confirms the Phase 5.A.2 diagnostic→playable transition did not break any of the 14 prior cross-runtime determinism assertions established in Phases 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B.
follow_ups_observations: live-domain-https-enforcement: porkchop.github.io/icefall/ continues to 301-redirect to http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/ (plaintext) — same observation carried forward from phase-3.B / 4.B. Custom-domain HTTPS enforcement is a Pages settings toggle and remains a Phase 9 release-hardening item; not a Phase 5.B blocker because the deployed bytes and cross-runtime e2e assertions are independent of transport (the CI Playwright job runs against http://127.0.0.1:4173/icefall/ via vite preview, not against the live URL).

deploy_step_duration: the deploy-pages step ran 4m 43s versus the typical 9-13s observed in Phases 4.A.2/4.B/4.A.1. The total workflow was 6m 55s vs typical 2-3 min. The `build & test` and `cross-os atlas equality` jobs ran in normal time (1m 56s and 19s-1m 10s respectively); the latency is GitHub Pages-side (likely propagation or the Pages CDN warming). Not a workflow defect; not a Phase 5.B blocker. If this latency persists in Phase 6+, consider monitoring.
follow_ups_carried_forward_from_phase_5_: Code-review N1: src/render/canvas.ts:150 has fillStyle = '#000' as a hidden constant; could be a named export.
Code-review N2: tests/ui/fake-dom.ts works for one consumer (151 lines, mini DOM stub) but Phase 6+ may push toward happy-dom or jsdom as a devDep if more layers need DOM tests.
Code-review N3: Direction cast in DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS in src/input/keyboard.ts could be a tiny helper.

Phase 3.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards still held back: applyFloorEntry redundant param, dirOrdinalForStep export comment, ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES shared constant, tick unknown-action defensive type-check, e2e SIM_DIGEST duplication. Address when those files are naturally touched (Phase 6+ when action vocabulary extends with inventory pickup actions).
next_phase: Phase 6 — Items, Currency, Equipment per docs/PHASES.md:355+. Goal: treasures, eddies, consumables (stim patches, trauma packs), and equippable cyberware/weapons that modify combat outcomes; atlas recipes extended to cover the full item set. Lead agent: engine-builder (rules) + frontend-builder (UI). Reviewers: code-reviewer + qa-playwright. Deliverables include: inventory and equipment data structures in sim; pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use action descriptors (extends the Phase 1 Action vocabulary additively); inventory and equipment screens in UI; item registry populated with ~20 starter items; atlas recipes for all ~20 items so atlas regenerates with new sprites (cross-OS matrix re-asserts byte equality on the new atlas binary). Acceptance criteria include: item effects deterministic and resolved through the same hash-driven combat path; inventory state fully reconstructible from the action log alone; atlas regenerates with new sprites and CI fails if checked-in atlas is stale; Playwright test on live URL pickup/equip/kill scripted monster on fixed seed asserts state hash matches a golden value. Phase 6 is NOT a planning-gate phase per docs/PHASES.md:7. The X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B decomposition is the established pattern from Phases 3 / 4 / 5; Phase 6's first sub-phase will be a drift-detection sweep + Phase 5.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards that touch sim files Phase 6 will modify. The Phase 6 atlas re-generation will exercise the cross-OS matrix on a new atlas binary — if it goes red on macOS or Windows, that's a deterministic-encoder regression and a phase-update event.
